metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

ECON-H002.title: Cross-Chain UUPS Upgrade Replay via executeWithoutChainIdValidation
ECON-H002.duplicate_group_description: Cross-chain replay of UUPS upgradeToAndCall via canSkipChainIdValidation whitelist allows wallet takeover or bricking on secondary chains where implementation address contains different or no code

TECH-H001.title: RIP-7212 Precompile False Positive on Chains with Arbitrary Code at Address 0x100
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: RIP-7212 precompile spoofing at hardcoded address 0x100 allows forged WebAuthn/passkey signatures on chains where a non-precompile contract exists at that address

TECH-H003.title: No Minimum Owner Count Allows Permanent Wallet Bricking via Owner Removal
TECH-H003.duplicate_group_description: removeOwnerAtIndex has no minimum owner count enforcement, allowing removal of all owners and permanent wallet bricking with no recovery path

ECON-H001.title: MagicSpend Paymaster Batch Balance Over-Commitment Leading to Solvency Violation
ECON-H001.duplicate_group_description: MagicSpend validatePaymasterUserOp performs per-UserOp balance check (address(this).balance >= withdrawAmount) without cumulative accounting, allowing multiple UserOps to over-commit the contract's ETH balance in the same bundle

ECON-H004.title: Uninitialized Proxy Takeover via Empty Owners Array in initialize()
ECON-H004.duplicate_group_description: CoinbaseSmartWallet.initialize() with empty owners array leaves nextOwnerIndex at 0, defeating the re-initialization guard and allowing anyone to call initialize() again with their own owners

ECON-H005.title: MagicSpend postOp Assert Failure Creates Unrecoverable Fund Loss
ECON-H005.duplicate_group_description: MagicSpend postOp assert(mode != PostOpMode.postOpReverted) creates unrecoverable failure mode where gas refund is permanently lost if ETH transfer fails

OPS2-H007.title: MagicSpend paymaster nonce-burning DoS: any EntryPoint caller can permanently invalidate signed withdraw requests by submitting failing UserOps
OPS2-H007.duplicate_group_description: MagicSpend nonce permanently consumed in _validateRequest before signature validity and expiry are enforced, enabling nonce-burning griefing

TECH-H010.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Owner Removal Enables Multi-Chain Wallet Bricking
TECH-H010.duplicate_group_description: Cross-chain replay of removeOwnerAtIndex with divergent owner indices across chains removes unintended owners, causing access loss or wallet bricking

TECH-H016.title: CoinbaseSmartWallet executeBatch unbounded calls array can be abused for gas-grief DoS against wallet operations
TECH-H016.duplicate_group_description: executeBatch iterates over unbounded user-controlled array with external calls, enabling gas-based DoS

TECH-H015.title: CoinbaseSmartWallet arbitrary-call reentrancy enables bypassing intended sequencing of owner changes/upgrade in the same execution context
TECH-H015.duplicate_group_description: execute/executeBatch perform arbitrary external calls with no reentrancy guard while msg.sender==address(this) is treated as authorized owner, enabling privilege escalation via callback or self-call

ECON2-H003.title: Physical Access Attack via Missing User Verification in WebAuthn
ECON2-H003.duplicate_group_description: WebAuthn.verify called with requireUV:false disables User Verification check, reducing passkey security to single-factor (physical presence only)

OPS-H013.title: MagicSpend EntryPoint funding is incorrectly implemented: entryPointDeposit() sends ETH to EntryPoint address instead of calling depositTo(), risking funds loss/non-accounting
OPS-H013.duplicate_group_description: MagicSpend.entryPointDeposit uses raw ETH transfer (safeTransferETH) to EntryPoint instead of calling depositTo(), potentially failing to credit the paymaster's deposit balance

OPS-H012.title: Direct withdraw() permits arbitrary ERC20 withdrawals even though paymaster path enforces ETH-only (asset confusion drain)
OPS-H012.duplicate_group_description: MagicSpend.withdraw() allows arbitrary ERC20 withdrawals (not restricted to ETH like the paymaster path), enabling drain of accidentally held tokens

ECON2-H006.title: FCL.ecAff_isOnCurve Accepts Non-Canonical P-256 Points Enabling Persistent Owner Backdoor
ECON2-H006.duplicate_group_description: Non-canonical P-256 public key coordinates (x+p, y) pass FCL.ecAff_isOnCurve but encode to different bytes, enabling persistent owner backdoor surviving removal via MultiOwnable's byte-level deduplication

OPS2-H001.title: Selective Cross-Chain Replay of Owner Management Enables Permanent Unauthorized Access
OPS2-H001.duplicate_group_description: Cross-chain replay of owner addition operations grants unintended global access to chain-specific delegates via selective or automatic replay

ECON2-H008.title: CoinbaseSmartWalletFactory.createAccount Permanently Locks ETH for Already-Deployed Accounts
ECON2-H008.duplicate_group_description: CoinbaseSmartWalletFactory.createAccount with msg.value for already-deployed accounts can lock or misdirect ETH

TECH-H002.title: Cross-Chain Replay of upgradeToAndCall Bricks Wallets on Unsynchronized Chains

TECH-H004.title: MagicSpend Cumulative Balance Over-Commitment via Multiple UserOps in Same Bundle

TECH-H005.title: Re-initialization of Wallet Deployed Without Factory Due to Empty Owner Array

TECH-H006.title: MagicSpend Nonce Permanently Consumed Before EntryPoint Expiry Enforcement in Paymaster Path

TECH-H007.title: MagicSpend postOp Assert Failure Causes Gas Refund Loss When ETH Transfer Fails

TECH-H008.title: WebAuthn authenticatorData Minimum Length Not Validated Before Flag Access

TECH-H009.title: FCL ecZZ_mulmuladd_S_asm Incorrect Point-at-Infinity Handling During Scalar Multiplication

TECH-H011.title: WebAuthn requireUV Hardcoded to False Reduces Passkey Security to User Presence Only

TECH-H012.title: MagicSpend withdraw() Expiry Check After Nonce Consumption Creates Atomic Griefing on Direct Withdraw Path

TECH-H013.title: MagicSpend solvency break: multiple validated UserOps can over-credit _withdrawableETH beyond ETH balance, enabling later withdrawal of future deposits

TECH-H014.title: MagicSpend DoS/value loss: entryPointDeposit uses plain ETH transfer to EntryPoint instead of depositTo, potentially locking funds and breaking sponsorship

TECH-H017.title: MultiOwnable initialization allows duplicate/invalid owners when passed malformed bytes that decode ambiguously across address/publicKey formats

TECH-H018.title: ERC1271 replaySafeHash domainSeparator recomputation per call may enable griefing via expensive signature checks (gas DoS) in external integrations

TECH-H019.title: FCL ecAff_add uses ecAff_IsZero(y==0) identity shortcut that could be exploitable if reused for non-P256 curves or non-prime order contexts

ECON-H003.title: RIP-7212 Precompile Address Spoofing Enables Forged WebAuthn Signatures

ECON-H006.title: MagicSpend _withdrawableETH Accumulation Across Multiple Paymaster UserOps Creates Unbalanced Payout

ECON-H007.title: All-Owner Removal Creates Permanent Irrecoverable Fund Lock

ECON-H008.title: MagicSpend Nonce Consumed Before Expiry Enforcement in Paymaster Path

ECON-H009.title: WebAuthn requireUV=false Reduces Passkey Security to Single-Factor

ECON-H010.title: MagicSpend Owner Can Drain Funds Committed to User _withdrawableETH Balances

ECON-H011.title: Cross-Chain Replay of UUPS Upgrade Allows Complete Wallet Takeover

ECON-H012.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Owner Removal Causes Permanent Access Loss via Index Desynchronization

ECON-H013.title: False Positive Signature Validation via Malicious RIP-7212 Precompile

ECON-H014.title: Paymaster Deposit Drain via Bundled UserOp Balance Check Bypass

ECON-H015.title: Passkey Authentication Downgraded to Single-Factor due to Disabled User Verification

ECON-H016.title: Permanent Fund Lockup via Complete Owner Removal

OPS-H001.title: MagicSpend Cumulative Balance Over-Commitment via Multiple UserOps in Same Bundle

OPS-H002.title: Cross-Chain Wallet Bricking via Upgrade Replay to Non-Existent Implementation

OPS-H003.title: WebAuthn Signature Forgery via Spoofed RIP-7212 Precompile on Non-Supporting Chains

OPS-H004.title: Permanent Fund Lock via Removal of All Wallet Owners (Missing Minimum Owner Count)

OPS-H005.title: Wallet Re-Initialization Takeover via Empty Owners Array in initialize()

OPS-H006.title: MagicSpend PostOp Assert Failure Causes User Fund Loss When ETH Transfer Fails

OPS-H007.title: MagicSpend Nonce Permanently Consumed on Expired or Invalid Paymaster Validation

OPS-H008.title: WebAuthn User Verification Bypass via Hardcoded requireUV=false

OPS-H009.title: MagicSpend Owner Can Drain Contract Including User-Allocated WithdrawableETH

OPS-H010.title: Any Single Owner Can Unilaterally Capture Wallet by Adding Self and Removing All Others

OPS-H011.title: Re-initialization takeover possible if wallet is initialized with an empty owners array (nextOwnerIndex stays 0)

OPS-H014.title: Unbounded owner index growth can create a gas-DoS vector for signature validation and owner management

OPS-H015.title: Factory createAccount can unintentionally fund already-deployed accounts with msg.value (confused-deputy funding loss)

OPS-H016.title: ERC1271 domain separator recomputation is not cached and may be used as a gas-grief vector in high-frequency signature checks

OPS-H017.title: FCL.ecAff_add / ecAff_IsZero treats any point with y==0 as identity, which is non-generic and dangerous if reused beyond P-256 assumptions

TECH2-H001.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Implementation Upgrades Can Brick Wallets or Allow Takeover

TECH2-H002.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Owner Removal Causes Unintended Lockouts Due to Index Divergence

TECH2-H003.title: MagicSpend Paymaster Violates ERC-7562 Storage Access Rules, Causing Bundler Rejection

TECH2-H004.title: Multiple UserOps in Same Bundle Can Bypass MagicSpend Balance Check

TECH2-H005.title: Unspent Gas Refund is Permanently Lost if postOp ETH Transfer Fails

TECH2-H006.title: RIP-7212 Precompile Fallback Can Be Spoofed on Unsupported Chains

TECH2-H007.title: Passkey Authentication Bypasses User Verification (UV), Reducing Security to Single-Factor

TECH2-H008.title: Wallet Can Be Permanently Bricked by Removing All Owners

TECH2-H009.title: CoinbaseSmartWalletFactory createAccount salt depends on raw owners bytes ordering; duplicate semantic owner sets can create multiple addresses, breaking user expectations and recovery tooling

ECON2-H001.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Wallet Upgrades Leads to Account Takeover

ECON2-H002.title: Cross-Chain Replay of Owner Management Causes Unauthorized Access

ECON2-H004.title: Permanent Account Lockout via Unrestricted Owner Removal

ECON2-H005.title: Bundler Griefing via Cumulative Balance Check Bypass in Paymaster

ECON2-H007.title: ERC1271 isValidSignature Validates Signatures Against Non-Canonical Keys Surviving Owner Removal

ECON2-H009.title: MultiOwnable.addOwnerPublicKey Lacks P-256 Curve Point Validation Enabling Invalid Owner Slot Consumption

ECON2-H010.title: MagicSpend._withdraw ERC20 Fee-on-Transfer Token Accounting Discrepancy

ECON2-H011.title: MagicSpend Admin Functions Can Drain ETH Committed to User _withdrawableETH Balances

OPS2-H002.title: Permanent Wallet Bricking via Complete Owner Removal

OPS2-H003.title: Uninitialized Proxy Takeover via Empty Owners Array

OPS2-H005.title: Single-Factor Authentication for Passkeys via Disabled User Verification

OPS2-H006.title: MagicSpend Admin Can Drain Funds Backing Valid User Withdrawals

OPS2-H008.title: MagicSpend solvency break via cumulative over-crediting: multiple validations can create unbacked withdrawable balances

OPS2-H009.title: MagicSpend direct withdraw allows arbitrary ERC20 withdrawals (not just ETH): signed requests can drain any tokens accidentally sent to contract

OPS2-H010.title: CoinbaseSmartWallet arbitrary call execution can be used for reentrancy-style self-call privilege chaining via msg.sender == address(this)

OPS2-H011.title: CoinbaseSmartWallet replay-safe ERC1271 hashing may break dApp expectations leading to unsafe fallback signing patterns

OPS2-H012.title: Factory determinism + open deployment enables address squatting and fund misdirection to predeployed accounts

OPS2-H013.title: FCL modular inversion via ModExp precompile: chain-specific precompile behavior or gas limits can cause signature validation outages

OPS2-H014.title: CoinbaseSmartWallet EOA-owner signature verification relies on SignatureCheckerLib: contract-based owners could introduce unexpected reverts during validateUserOp
